body_txt: Lots of characters have a Dark and Troubled Past , often sharing them in a Somber Backstory Revelation . People cry. Hugs are offered. People might be moved to a Heel Realization or a Jerkass Realization by learning they've been harming someone who has been through so much anguish. And then there's this person. They have a Dark and Troubled Past ... from the perspective of the listener. The way they tell it, you'd think it was a fond memory of a happy time in their life or just something completely normal that they're not at all concerned with. It could be that they've been Conditioned to Accept Horror . They might be a Cloudcuckoolander who honestly believes that the very disturbing details they just so cheerfully shared are perfectly normal for everyone. They might even think that it's something everyone else has undergone, so they see no reason to make a big deal of it and might discuss it as casually as they discuss the weather. Or they innocently misunderstand what happened. The person may be relaying horrific details from their past in a sort of " When I Was Your Age... " kind of way, suggesting they're proud of what they've experienced. But the people who hear their story are horrified . Expect slack-jawed blank stares or looks of alarm and concern, which the person relaying the story will be puzzled by. If a character isn't alarmed by these sorts of revelations, it could serve as Foreshadowing that something is off with them as well. In any case, whether or not the other characters respond with shock or alarm to such revelations, expect the audience to do so. Typically Played for Laughs , usually of the Black Comedy variety. May have overlap with Hilariously Abusive Childhood . Also, compare Stepford Smiler , where a person may be putting on a cheerful front to cover up the deep-seated issues caused by their traumatic past. Also compare The Pollyanna . See also Angst? What Angst? . Delusions of Parental Love is quite similar. Often Truth in Television , as many abused children will be Conditioned to Accept Horror . More than one survivor of childhood trauma has only learned that what they went through isn't normal in adulthood, when they tell what they think is a funny story about their childhood only to find their friends staring in horror. Compare and contrast Trauma Deduction . Contrast Somber Backstory Revelation , where someone discusses their tragic backstory in a more expected, and unhappy sort of way. Sub-Trope of Dissonant Serenity . No Real Life Examples, Please!
